heading: Merchant mariner recruitment, training, and retention strategic plan
label: 51707 Merchant mariner recruitment, training, and retention strategic plan
notes: References in TextThe date of the enactment of this section, referred to in subsec. (a)(1), is the date of enactment of , which was approved .Pub. L. 116–283Jan. 1, 2021

children.heading: 1So in original. No subsec. (b) has been enacted.1Strategic Plan.—
children.label: a 1So in original. No subsec. (b) has been enacted.1Strategic Plan.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Not later than one year after the date of the enactment of this section, and at least once every five years thereafter until the termination date under paragraph (6), the Secretary of Transportation, acting through the Administrator of the Maritime Administration, shall publish in the Federal Register a plan to recruit, train, and retain merchant mariners for the five-year period following the date of publication of the most recently published plan under this paragraph.

children.label: A
children.text: a strategy to address merchant mariner recruitment, training, and retention issues in the United States; and

children.label: B
children.text: demonstration and research priorities concerning merchant mariner recruitment, training, and retention.

children.label: A
children.text: the availability of existing research (as of the date of publication of the plan); and

children.label: B
children.text: the need to ensure results that have broad applicability for the United States merchant marine workforce development.

children.heading: .—Consultation
children.label: 4 .—Consultation
children.text: In developing a plan under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall consult with representatives of the maritime industry, labor organizations, including the Commander of the Transportation Command and the Commander of the Military Sealift Command, and other governmental entities and stakeholders in the maritime industry.

children.heading: .—Transmittal to congress
children.label: 5 .—Transmittal to congress
children.text: The Secretary shall transmit copies of any plan published under paragraph (1) to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate.

children.heading: .—Termination date
children.label: 6 .—Termination date
children.text: The requirement to publish a plan under this paragraph shall terminate on the date that the Administrator of the Maritime Administration determines that there is an adequate number of United States mariners for sustained strategic sealift.
